history.note: Created by local Cybrus automated planning for task 7E902F13-AAEA-4A8E-A430-8F3E013C4D1D.

Current code has package manifests, required connector metadata, setup_required statuses, and /start email linking fallback. Missing: install_chief_of_staff shared path, connector_readiness/2, TelegramLink.deep_link/1, dashboard install handler/section, docs/install-chief-of-staff.md, self-serve smoke test.
Starting implementation after completing the proactive delivery planner spec. Work will stay in progress until the self-serve install flow is implemented, verified, and moved to done.

Implemented connector readiness, shared Chief of Staff install path, dashboard install row, Telegram signed token linking with email fallback, de-Kent morning briefing defaults, docs, and smoke coverage. mix precommit passed with 1835 tests and 0 failures.

next_action: None for this spec. Pick the next ready Spectacula spec.

resume_context.last_completed_step: Implemented and verified the self-serve Chief of Staff install flow; final spec review passed on 2026-05-20.
resume_context.notes: Dashboard install supports enabled and setup_required paths. Telegram signed start tokens are accepted; Telegram deep links fall back to the documented /start email path when the signed token exceeds Telegram start-param limits. Fresh tenant briefing defaults no longer bake in Kent/Runner commercial constants; primary admin config preserves those values only for the primary admin install.

summary: Done: self-serve Chief of Staff install now gates on Telegram/Gmail/Calendar readiness, records setup_required when incomplete, installs enabled/running when ready, links to connector setup from the dashboard, supports signed Telegram linking with email fallback, and includes docs and smoke tests.
title: Spec: Self-serve install flow for Chief of Staff agent

verification.notes: Ran mix precommit after targeted verification. The first full run exposed two real test issues: a morning briefing test still assuming global Runner commercial defaults, and an LLM retry test cleanup race. Updated the tests to match the new config-backed behavior and ExUnit-supervised process lifecycle, then reran mix precommit successfully.
